We earn a small commission when you buy through them, at no extra cost to you. As an Amazon Associate we earn from qualifying purchases. See our ",["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/disclaimer","className":"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline","children":"disclaimer"}]," for details."]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","h2","0",{"id":"why-a-converter-instead-of-a-full-standing-desk","children":"Why a Converter Instead of a Full Standing Desk"}],["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A full electric standing desk runs $400 to $1,200 and replaces your existing desk entirely. A standing desk converter sits <em>on top of</em> your current desk, raises a smaller surface up and down, and runs $150 to $400. For most home-office workers — especially renters, apartment-dwellers, or anyone with a perfectly good desk already — the converter is the better economic and practical choice."}}],"$L9","$La","$Lb","$Lc","$Ld","$Le","$Lf","$L10","$L11","$L12","$L13","$L14","$L15","$L16","$L17","$L18","$L19","$L1a","$L1b","$L1c","$L1d","$L1e","$L1f","$L20","$L21","$L22","$L23","$L24","$L25","$L26","$L27","$L28","$L29","$L2a","$L2b","$L2c","$L2d","$L2e","$L2f","$L30","$L31","$L32","$L33","$L34","$L35","$L36"]}],"$L37","$L38","$L39"]}],"$L3a"]}]]}]
7:["$","script","script-0",{"src":"/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js","async":true}]
8:["$","$L3b",null,{"children":["$","$3c",null,{"name":"Next.MetadataOutlet","children":"$@3d"}]}]
9:["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The trade-off: converters offer less workspace at standing height than a full standing desk, the keyboard tray sits at a fixed offset from the monitor, and the up-down mechanism is usually manual (gas spring or X-lift) instead of motorized."}}]
a:["$","p","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For 90% of users, that's an acceptable trade. Here are the converters worth buying in 2026."}}]
b:["$","h2","4",{"id":"what-to-look-for","children":"What to Look For"}]
c:["$","p","5",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Five things matter:"}}]
d:["$","ul","6",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Surface area</strong> — the keyboard tray needs to fit your full keyboard <em>and</em> a mouse with elbow room. Aim for at least 30\" wide on the keyboard level. The monitor surface above can be smaller."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Lift mechanism</strong> — gas-spring (smoothest, single-handle), X-lift (decent, two-squeeze release), or electric (motorized, more expensive). Manual is fine for most."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Standing height range</strong> — your elbow at 90° when standing should land on the keyboard tray. Most converters max out at 16–19\" of lift. Tall users (over 6'2\") should look for 19\"+."}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Stability</strong> — when fully raised, the converter shouldn't wobble when you type. Cheap converters fail here. Check reviews specifically for \"wobble at full height.\""}}],["$","li","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Footprint when collapsed</strong> — when you sit, the converter is still on your desk. Keep total depth under 22\" or it'll dominate a small surface."}}]]}]
e:["$","h2","7",{"id":"top-picks-for-2026","children":"Top Picks for 2026"}]
f:["$","h3","8",{"id":"1-vivo-black-standing-desk-converter-best-overall","children":"1. Vivo Black Standing Desk Converter — Best Overall"}]
10:["$","p","9",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The category benchmark for years. Solid build, smooth gas-spring lift, two-tier surface (monitor on top, keyboard below). Available in 32\" and 36\" widths."}}]
11:["$","ul","10",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Width:</strong> 32\" or 36\""}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Lift:</strong> Gas spring, single-squeeze handle"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Height range:</strong> 6.5\" to 16.5\""}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Stability:</strong> Excellent — minimal wobble even at full height"}}],["$","li","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Price:</strong> ~$160 (32\"), ~$200 (36\")"}}]]}]
12:["$","p","11",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"If you want one converter and don't want to think about it, this."}}]
13:["$","h3","12",{"id":"2-flexispot-m7-best-for-larger-setups","children":"2. Flexispot M7 — Best for Larger Setups"}]
14:["$","p","13",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For users with 27\"+ monitors or dual-monitor setups. Surface is larger (35\" wide) and the keyboard tray is removable for sit-mode flexibility."}}]
15:["$","ul","14",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Width:</strong> 35\""}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Lift:</strong> Gas spring"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Height range:</strong> 4.7\" to 19.7\""}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Stability:</strong> Very good"}}],["$","li","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Price:</strong> ~$240"}}]]}]
16:["$","p","15",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The 19.7\" lift range matters if you're tall. Most cheap converters top out at 16\", which leaves 6'+ users with their arms slightly raised."}}]
17:["$","h3","16",{"id":"3-varidesk-pro-plus-36-best-build-quality","children":"3. Varidesk Pro Plus 36 — Best Build Quality"}]
18:["$","p","17",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Vari makes the most premium converter on the market. Stiffer chassis than competitors, integrated keyboard tray, more refined finish. Trade-off is price."}}]
19:["$","ul","18",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Width:</strong> 36\""}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Lift:</strong> Spring assist (heavier action than Vivo)"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Height range:</strong> Up to 17.5\""}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Stability:</strong> Best in class"}}],["$","li","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Price:</strong> ~$395"}}]]}]
1a:["$","p","19",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"If you'll be raising and lowering 4–6 times a day for years, the Vari justifies its price by lasting longer than 2–3 cycles of cheaper converters."}}]
1b:["$","h3","20",{"id":"4-mount-it-mi-7926-best-budget-pick","children":"4. Mount-It! MI-7926 — Best Budget Pick"}]
1c:["$","p","21",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"If $200 is too much, the Mount-It at half the price gets you 70% of the experience. Smaller surface, slightly more wobble, but functional."}}]
1d:["$","ul","22",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Width:</strong> 32\""}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Lift:</strong> X-lift, two-handle squeeze"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Height range:</strong> 6\" to 16.7\""}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Stability:</strong> Moderate (some wobble at full height with heavy monitors)"}}],["$","li","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Price:</strong> ~$95"}}]]}]
1e:["$","p","23",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Good \"test if I'll actually use this\" purchase. Upgrade to a Vivo if you find yourself standing more than expected."}}]
1f:["$","h3","24",{"id":"5-apexdesk-vortex-best-for-standing-desk-skeptics","children":"5. ApexDesk Vortex — Best for Standing-Desk Skeptics"}]
20:["$","p","25",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The Vortex is the most \"sit-mode friendly\" converter on the market. Its keyboard tray slides completely under the monitor surface when not in standing mode, making it nearly invisible at sit height."}}]
21:["$","ul","26",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Width:</strong> 36\""}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Lift:</strong> X-lift"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Height range:</strong> 5\" to 16\""}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Stability:</strong> Good"}}],["$","li","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Price:</strong> ~$180"}}]]}]
22:["$","p","27",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"If your concern is \"I don't want a giant block of converter on my desk all day even when I'm sitting,\" this design solves that."}}]
23:["$","h2","28",{"id":"setup-tips-for-small-spaces","children":"Setup Tips for Small Spaces"}]
24:["$","ul","29",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Measure your sit-mode workspace first.</strong> Your converter sits on your desk permanently. If your desk is 48\"×24\", a 36\" converter takes up 75% of the depth."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Center the converter, don't push it back.</strong> Pushing it against the wall puts the monitor too far from your eyes when standing. Center alignment keeps eye-to-screen distance correct in both modes."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Add an <a href=\"/blog/best-anti-fatigue-mats-standing-desk\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">anti-fatigue mat</a> underneath</strong> — non-negotiable. Standing on hard floor for hours is what kills the standing-desk experience."}}]]}]
25:["$","h2","30",{"id":"when-not-to-buy-a-converter","children":"When NOT to Buy a Converter"}]
26:["$","p","31",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A converter is the wrong choice if:"}}]
27:["$","ul","32",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>You have a small desk (under 36\" wide)</strong> — the converter will eat the entire surface"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>You'll stand more than 50% of the time</strong> — at high standing percentages, the manual lift/lower routine gets old. Get a full electric standing desk."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>You use a desktop tower with monitors mounted on a separate stand</strong> — the converter mostly addresses laptop and small-monitor setups"}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Your desk is already height-adjustable</strong> — buying a converter is redundant"}}]]}]
28:["$","p","33",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For full standing-desk picks and proper sit/stand schedules, see our <a href=\"/blog/standing-desk-guide\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">standing desk guide</a>."}}]
29:["$","h2","34",{"id":"how-to-use-a-converter-correctly","children":"How to Use a Converter Correctly"}]
2a:["$","p","35",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A converter only helps if you actually stand. The mistake most people make: buy the converter, use it once a week, eventually stop. To make standing a habit:"}}]
2b:["$","ul","36",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Start with a 25/75 schedule.</strong> Stand 15 minutes every hour. Let your body adapt for the first 2 weeks."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Move to 50/50.</strong> After adaptation, alternate hour-on, hour-off."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Default the converter to standing height.</strong> Make sitting the active choice rather than standing — friction reverses motivation."}}]]}]
2c:["$","h2","37",{"id":"frequently-asked-questions","children":"Frequently Asked Questions"}]
2d:["$","p","38",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Will a converter damage my desk?</strong>"}}]
2e:["$","p","39",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Most converters weigh 35–50 lbs and have rubber-padded feet. Any sturdy desk handles them. Glass desks are the exception — verify weight rating before placing a converter on glass."}}]
2f:["$","p","40",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Can I use a converter with two monitors?</strong>"}}]
30:["$","p","41",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Yes for two 24\" monitors side-by-side on most 35\"+ converters. Three monitors is pushing it; a full standing desk is the better fit."}}]
31:["$","p","42",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Do converters work on standing desks?</strong>"}}]
32:["$","p","43",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Defeats the purpose. If you have a height-adjustable desk, you don't need a converter."}}]
33:["$","p","44",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>How long does the gas spring last?</strong>"}}]
34:["$","p","45",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Quality converters (Vivo, Flexispot, Varidesk) are rated for 20,000+ cycles — 5–10 years of daily use. Budget converters often need spring replacement at year 2–3."}}]
35:["$","p","46",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Can I use any monitor arm with a converter?</strong>"}}]
36:["$","p","47",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Some — check whether your converter has a desk-clamp area at the back. Vivo and Flexispot are arm-friendly; Varidesk Pro Plus is not."}}]
